summary: Test suite covers core requirements: glob pattern matching, multiple labels per file, priority ordering, and deduplication are all tested. Includes workflow structure validation and end-to-end integration tests via act with 3 test cases. However, several gaps limit confidence: (1) Glob patterns—only `?` is explicitly tested; `**`, `**/prefix`, `suffix/**`, and complex patterns like `**/*.test.*` are not tested despite being central to the task. (2) CLI coverage incomplete—`--files-file` argument is never tested, only `--files-json`. (3) Edge cases missing—empty inputs, whitespace handling, special characters in paths/labels, very large priority values, and concurrent label production across multiple files with different priorities. (4) Error scenarios—error message content is not validated, only exit codes and exceptions. (5) Fixtures—temporary files use `/tmp` with no cleanup, risking race conditions. Test organization is clean, naming is clear, and independence is good, but depth is insufficient. Would catch obvious regressions in the happy path but might miss subtle bugs in glob handling, CLI argument variants, and edge cases.
